title: Medusa Makes a Choice
tagline: Someone needed help. Someone Medusa didn't expect to care about. She cared anyway. She will not discuss this.
arc_note: MEDUSA ARC — KEY MILESTONE. Medusa becomes reluctant ally. Not a full turn — she helps once, for specific reasons, and makes clear she has not changed her fundamental nature. But she helped. That's the crack in the wall becoming a door.

characters.focus: Mini Medusa — her choice is the episode
characters.crisis: Baby Hercules got lost in the canyon between Olympus and Medusa's valley. He's fine physically — the canyon can't hurt Hercules — but he's been there for four hours and can't find his way out because he keeps taking the widest path (wrong direction) and the canyon keeps getting narrower.
characters.medusa_role: She can hear him from her valley. She's been hearing him for two hours. She knew where he was. She waited. Then she got up and went to help. She will provide no explanation for this.

scenes.title: Hercules Is Missing
scenes.narration: Baby Hercules had been exploring. This was known to be a risk. He had been exploring for four hours, which was past the point where exploring becomes lost. The group had split to search — Zeus toward the north ridge, Perseus toward the sea, Achilles covering everything at speed. Nobody had checked the canyon. The canyon didn't seem like somewhere Hercules would get stuck. The canyon turned out to be exactly somewhere Hercules would get stuck.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Search montage — Zeus north, Perseus at sea, Achilles in sprint-blur. The canyon in the background: narrow, maze-like. From deep inside it, barely visible: Baby Hercules's lion cape. He's fine. He's just going the wrong way every time. 16:9.

scenes.title: From the Valley
scenes.narration: Medusa had been working in her garden when she first heard him — approximately two hours ago. She had recognized the sound: a small child, not in danger, just thoroughly lost, calling out occasionally. She had kept working. She had added three new sculptures. She had reorganized the owl section. She had sat down. She had stood up. She had looked at the canyon wall. She had kept working. She was still listening.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Medusa in her garden, working. But her snakes are all tilted toward the canyon wall — listening. She's adding sculptures. Then reorganizing. Then sitting. Then standing. She keeps looking at the canyon wall. Trying not to go. Her snakes — especially Dreamy — looking between her and the wall. 16:9.

scenes.title: She Goes
scenes.narration: She went. She didn't announce it to her snakes. She didn't explain it. She pulled her sunglasses down firmly and walked into the canyon entrance from the valley side. She navigated by sound — Hercules calling out occasionally, always from the wrong direction. She reached him in eleven minutes. She said: 'You're going the wrong way. Every time.' He looked up at her. He said: 'Hi!' She said: 'Do not look directly at my face.' He looked at his feet. She said: 'Follow my voice.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Medusa walking into the canyon — sunglasses firmly down, navigating purposefully. Finding Hercules, who is indeed fine and was going wrong every time. His cheerful 'Hi!' Her immediate instruction. He looks at his feet immediately, obedient. She speaks. He follows. The canyon behind them. 16:9.

scenes.title: What They Talk About
scenes.narration: The walk out took eight minutes. Hercules talked the entire time — about his lion cape, about a bird he'd seen, about breakfast. Medusa said almost nothing. Then Hercules said: 'Perseus talks about you.' She said: 'Does he.' It wasn't a question. Hercules said: 'He says your garden is the best thing he's ever seen.' She didn't reply for a moment. Then she said: 'The west section is the best. He hasn't seen the west section.' Hercules said: 'I'll tell him.' She said: 'Yes. You do that.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Medusa leading Hercules through the canyon — she navigating perfectly, he following her voice with eyes on his feet, talking constantly. The exchange about Perseus. Her 'he hasn't seen the west section' — said away from Hercules, almost to herself. Her snakes soft at this. Almost like she wanted Perseus to know. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Canyon Edge
scenes.narration: At the canyon exit — the Olympus side — Medusa stopped. She couldn't go further without sunlight risk to anyone on the Olympus paths. She said: 'You know the way from here.' Hercules turned around — eyes still on his feet. He said: 'Thank you!' very loudly and genuinely. She said: 'Don't mention it.' He said: 'I'm going to mention it to everyone.' She said: 'I know. Go.' He went. She stood at the canyon entrance for a moment. Then she went back.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Canyon exit — Medusa stopping at the boundary of safe ground. Her instruction. Hercules's genuine 'THANK YOU' — loud and real. Her 'don't mention it' and knowing he absolutely will. His departure. Her standing at the canyon edge a moment before turning back. Not quite smiling. Something near it. 16:9.

scenes.title: Hercules Mentions It
scenes.narration: Hercules mentioned it immediately, loudly, to everyone simultaneously. Zeus's reaction: genuine emotion. Perseus's reaction: very still, then very quietly: 'She showed him where the west section is?' Athena's reaction: one chin tap. Three taps. She opened her scroll to the section labeled MEDUSA. She made a note. Archie looked at the camera.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Hercules announcing to the assembled group. Reactions: Zeus's genuine emotion. Perseus going still. Athena making a note in her Medusa scroll section — three chin taps before the note. Archie looking at the camera. The group knowing something has shifted. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Did We Learn?
scenes.narration: Caring about someone is sometimes something that happens before you decide to. Medusa didn't decide to care about Hercules — she just found herself going into the canyon. That's often how it starts: you're doing something else, and you realize you've already started caring. See you next time on Little Olympus!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Final card — the group together, warm. In a small inset corner: Medusa back in her garden, adding one more sculpture to the west section. She's preparing something for when Perseus visits again. She would not call it preparation. 16:9.

lesson: Sometimes you care about people before you realize you've decided to. That's not weakness — that's just what caring is.
medusa_arc_note: EP014: Medusa helps without being asked, without explanation, without requiring recognition. She is a reluctant ally — not reformed, not changed, just cracked further open. EP020 (finale): she helps again, in a bigger way, when it matters most.
youtube_title: Medusa Saves Baby Hercules?! 💜😱 | Little Olympus Episode 14
next_episode_preview: Episode 15 — The Titan Warning! The ground shook again. And this time it didn't stop for three days.
